title: hydrangea vulgaris var. cordata
intro: Hydrangea vulgaris var. cordata, also known as the panicle hydrangea, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the Hydrangeaceae family, characterized by its broad, heart-shaped leaves and panicle-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hydrangea vulgaris var. cordata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate joint pain. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal purposes, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
